implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: Near-perfect implementation of a trivially-scoped spec. All acceptance criteria met in 3 lines; the dynamic year, copyright symbol, and 'BrewBoard' text are all present. Styling is tasteful and consistent with a Tailwind/stone palette. Only minor deductions: the footer is placed inside <main> rather than as a sibling (mild semantic HTML concern), and the implementation log is empty boilerplate.

requirements.notes: Both acceptance criteria satisfied: footer renders at the bottom of the page, contains '© {currentYear} BrewBoard'. No tests, docs, or refactoring added. Placed in the exact file called out by the technical approach.

correctness.notes: JSX is valid; `©` is legal in JSX; `{new Date().getFullYear()}` correctly computes the year at render time. Tailwind classes are syntactically valid. No logic errors.

minimality.notes: 3-line diff to page.tsx plus a workflow log file. Zero extraneous changes, no component extraction, no new files beyond the required Aigon log.

code_quality.notes: Semantic `<footer>` element, consistent stone palette, readable single line. Minor: the implementation log is entirely empty (all section headers blank) — the workflow artifact exists but carries no useful content.

risk.notes: Footer is a child of `<main>` rather than a sibling at the document level — technically incorrect HTML semantics, though functionally harmless in Next.js and common in practice. No accessibility or layout regressions introduced.
